title: The Blue Orb in Ep 26
wikitext:
[]: The Blue Orb in Ep. 26 Post by BeoX2 » Sun Aug 08, 2010 12:28 am In the "Congratulations!" scene in Ep. 26, Shinji and the gang are all standing on a big blue orb. I just thought that this may be symbolic of Shinji's "bubble" that he lives in, and now he's outside of it and ready to see the real world.

[]: Post by Duke » Sun Aug 08, 2010 12:55 am I thought it was supposed to be Earth

[]: Post by berto » Sun Aug 08, 2010 2:21 am They are in instrumentality so they could be inside the sea of LCL which is between the Earth and the Moon, the idea that it should be red would contrast with the emotions they want to project in that scene.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Aug 08, 2010 2:46 am It's the nearest thing to stepping out into the Big Blue Room that the trans-Complemented state affords.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Aug 08, 2010 2:46 pm I'm gonna go with the Earth idea.

[]: Post by NemZ » Sun Aug 08, 2010 5:55 pm It can't be Earth. The scale is WAY off and the 'ground' is made of coral (which live in colonies, thus is a hint that they're still in instrumentality).

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sun Aug 08, 2010 6:34 pm NemZ wrote: It can't be Earth. The scale is WAY off and the 'ground' is made of coral (which live in colonies, thus is a hint that they're still in instrumentality).

[]: Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Aug 08, 2010 9:18 pm Duke wrote: I thought it was supposed to be Earth

[]: Post by Hoshikawa-kun » Sun Aug 08, 2010 10:24 pm Possibly it's what the Earth looks like after 3I's apocalypse. Definitely a big blue ball. And probably the last dregs of the budget.

[]: Post by Ramiel » Sun Aug 08, 2010 11:11 pm Hoshikawa-kun wrote: And probably the last dregs of the budget.

[]: Post by thewayneiac » Mon Aug 09, 2010 8:58 am It's not all that hard to understand. The theater shattering and revealing a blue Earth and sky is the same sequence as the Black Moon shattering and dropping the souls back down to Earth. They've been freed from the oneness of Instrumentality, but they have yet to return to reality in that none of them has reembodied yet (Shinji has only just then rejected Instrumentality), hence the weird setting.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 09, 2010 2:25 pm NemZ wrote: It can't be Earth. The scale is WAY off and the 'ground' is made of coral (which live in colonies, thus is a hint that they're still in instrumentality).

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 09, 2010 11:57 pm thewayneiac wrote: The theater shattering and revealing a blue Earth and sky is the same sequence as the Black Moon shattering and dropping the souls back down to Earth.

[]: Post by NemZ » Tue Aug 10, 2010 4:39 am Sure is getting rather snippy up in here... but I guess that's to be expected when the sad old concurrency theory gets dragged out into the light yet again. Frankly, I'm bored with this argument.

[]: Post by esselfortium » Tue Aug 10, 2010 11:27 am NemZ wrote: Sure is getting rather snippy up in here... but I guess that's to be expected when the sad old concurrency theory gets dragged out into the light yet again. Frankly, I'm bored with this argument.

[]: Post by NemZ » Tue Aug 10, 2010 12:40 pm esselfortium wrote: You're surprised that people are debating your matter-of-fact statement of an unpopular theory as fact? :-\

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Aug 10, 2010 2:35 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I'm not sure I buy that. I've always felt that EoTV definitely ends while still in Instrumentality, and if the theater shattering was concurrent with the Black Moon shattering then everyone wouldn't still all be together congratulating Shinji.

[]: Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Aug 10, 2010 2:35 pm I'm with Nemz on the concurrency thing. I even abandon them once the debate starts. Obligatory You Are (NOT) Alone

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Aug 10, 2010 2:39 pm TehDonutKing wrote: I'm with Nemz on the concurrency thing. I even abandon them once the debate starts.

[]: Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Aug 10, 2010 2:40 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Can't we all just get along?
